title: Comforting Canadian Beef Wrap
description: A hearty and savory dish featuring tender beef, crispy naan bread, and a rich pasta water emulsion, all coming together in a nostalgic Canadian-inspired wrap.
instructions: Preheat a large skillet over medium high heat and cook the ground beef until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Remove the cooked beef from the skillet and set it aside on a plate.
In the same skillet, add the diced onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
In a small bowl, whisk together the pasta water and all purpose flour to make a slurry.
Add the slurry to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes, until it thickens and forms a rich emulsion.
Add the beef broth to the skillet and stir to combine, bringing the mixture to a simmer.
Return the cooked beef to the skillet and stir to combine with the emulsion.
Butter the naan bread pieces on the outside and cook them in the skillet for 1-2 minutes on each side, until crispy and golden brown.
Assemble the wraps by spooning the beef and emulsion mixture onto the naan bread and serving immediately.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F.
